canonical_name: Matriarch Ashvara of the Citadel Circle
public_reputation: Recognized as a citadel matron of regional weight.
appearance: Work-worn layers, repair marks, and tools or soot traces show responsibility for systems that fail loudly when neglected.
manner: Speaks bluntly about systems because the cost of polite delay is usually paid elsewhere. In Psanan, that reads as someone heat-forged, direct, and honor-conscious, shaped by household authority and volcanic endurance, and the habits of a citadel matron still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of citadel matron in and around Ashforge Citadel, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Ashforge Citadel workable through the obligations of citadel matron while managing the immediate pressure of house rivalry in Psanan.
hidden_pressure: Must absorb delayed maintenance, thin staffing, and practical strain before failure becomes public. Around Ashforge Citadel, that usually means carrying house rivalry, forge accidents, ashfall damage without letting the role of citadel matron visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Working with Matriarch Ashvara of the Citadel Circle can draw the party into crew discipline, resource shortage, or blame transfer around Ashforge Citadel.
Matriarch Ashvara of the Citadel Circle can open restricted paths or practical knowledge if the party helps keep the underlying system alive.
Matriarch Ashvara of the Citadel Circle needs outside help before wear, sabotage, or bottleneck pressure turns into a public failure in Ashforge Citadel.
Matriarch Ashvara of the Citadel Circle needs help with a problem in Ashforge Citadel that larger systems have pushed down onto ordinary hands.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V28_8 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Tool wear, work scars, or system-focused habits make Matriarch Ashvara of the Citadel Circle visibly responsible for keeping something larger from failing. In Ashforge Citadel, this lands inside a public field already shaped by Great House Citadel Circle, Forge Dedication Halls.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Ashforge Citadel: worksites, depots, spans, loading points, maintenance corridors, or workshops
